volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Self-Tormentor (Section 2.2.241b - Section 2.3.320)

sections.label: Section 2.2.241b
sections.original: Respira : eccum Dromonem cum Syro : una adsunt tibi .
sections.translation: Take courage . Look , here comes Dromo , together with Syrus : they are close at hand .

sections.label: Section 2.3.242
sections.translation: Do you say so ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.242b
sections.translation: ’ Tis as I told you ,—

sections.label: Section 2.3.242c
sections.translation: but in the mean time , while we ’ ve been carrying on our discourse , these women have been left behind .

sections.label: Section 2.3.243b
sections.translation: Don ’ t you hear , Clinia ? Your mistress is close at hand .

sections.label: Section 2.3.244
sections.original: Ego vero audio nunc demum et video et valeo , Clitipho .
sections.translation: Why yes , I do hear now at last , and I see and revive , Clitipho .

sections.label: Section 2.3.245
sections.original: Minime mirum , adeo impeditae sunt : ancillarum gregem
sections.translation: No wonder ; they are so encumbered ; they are bringing a troop of female attendants [*] with them .

sections.label: Section 2.3.246b
sections.translation: I ’ m undone ! Whence come these female attendants ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.246c
sections.translation: Do you ask me ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.247
sections.original: Non oportuit relictas : portant quid rerum !
sections.translation: We ought not to have left them ; what a quantity of things they are bringing !

sections.label: Section 2.3.247b
sections.translation: Ah me !

sections.label: Section 2.3.248
sections.original: Aurum , vestem : et vesperascit , et non noverunt viam .
sections.translation: Jewels of gold , and clothes ; it ’ s growing late too , and they don ’ t know the way . It was very foolish of us to leave them . Just go back , Dromo , and meet them . Make haste — why do you delay ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.250b
sections.original: Vae misero mihi , quanta de spe decidi !
sections.translation: Woe unto wretched me !— from what high hopes am I fallen !

sections.label: Section 2.3.251
sections.original: Quid istuc ? quae res te sollicitat autem ?
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ? Why , what is it that troubles you ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.251b
sections.translation: Do you ask what it is ? Why , don ’ t you see ? Attendants , jewels of gold , and clothes . her too , whom I left here with only one little servant girl . Whence do you suppose that they come ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.253b
sections.translation: Oh ! now at last I understand you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.254
sections.original: Di boni , quid turbae est ? aedes nostrae vix capient , scio .
sections.translation: Good Gods ! what a multitude there is ! Our house will hardly hold them , I ’ m sure .

sections.label: Section 2.3.255
sections.original: Quid comedent ? quid ebibent ? quid sene erit nostro miserius ?
sections.translation: How much they will eat ! how much they will drink ! what will there be more wretched than our old gentleman ? ( Catching sight of CLINIA and CLITIPHO .) But look , I espy the persons I was wanting .

sections.label: Section 2.3.256b
sections.translation: Oh Jupiter ! Why , where is fidelity gone ? While I , distractedly wandering , have abandoned my country for your sake , you , in the mean time , Antiphila , have been enriching yourself , and have forsaken me in these troubles , you for whose sake I am in extreme disgrace , and have been disobedient to my father ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.260
sections.original: Cuius nunc pudet me et miseret , qui harum mores cantabat mihi
sections.translation: on whose account I am now ashamed and grieved , that he who used to lecture me about the manners of these women , advised me in vain , and was not able to wean me away from her :— which , however , I shall now do ; whereas when it might have been advantageous to me to do so , I was unwilling . There is no being more wretched than I .

sections.label: Section 2.3.263b
sections.original: Hic de nostris verbis errat videlicet
sections.translation: He certainly has been misled by our words which we have been speaking here . ( Aloud .) Clinia , you imagine your mistress quite different from what she really is .

sections.label: Section 2.3.265
sections.original: Nam et vita est eadem , et animus te erga idem ac fuit ;
sections.translation: For both her mode of life is the same , and her disposition toward you is the same as it always was ; so far as we could form a judgment from the circumstances themselves .

sections.label: Section 2.3.267
sections.original: Quid est , obsecro ? nam mihi nunc nihil rerum omnium est
sections.translation: How so , prithee ? For nothing in the world could I rather wish for just now , than that I have suspected this without reason .

sections.label: Section 2.3.269
sections.original: Hoc primum , ut ne quid huius rerum ignores ; anus
sections.translation: This , in the first place , then ( that you may not be ignorant of any thing that concerns her ); the old woman ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.270
sections.original: Quae est dicta mater esse ei antehac non fuit :
sections.translation: who was formerly said to be her mother , was not so .— She is dead : this I overheard by accident from her , as we came along , while she was telling the other one .

sections.label: Section 2.3.272b
sections.translation: Pray , who is the other one ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.273
sections.original: Mane : hoc quod coepi primum enarrem , Clitipho :
sections.translation: Stay ; what I have begun I wish first to relate , Clitipho ; I shall come to that afterward .

sections.label: Section 2.3.274b
sections.translation: Make haste , then .

sections.label: Section 2.3.274c
sections.translation: First of all then ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.275
sections.original: Ubi ventum ad aedes est , Dromo pultat fores :
sections.translation: when we came to the house , Dromo knocked at the door ; a certain old woman came out ; when she opened the door , he directly rushed in ; I followed ; the old woman bolted the door , and returned to her wool . On this occasion might be known , Clinia , or else on none ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.280
sections.original: Quo studio vitam suam te absente exegerit ,
sections.translation: in what pursuits she passed her life during your absence ; when we thus came upon a female unexpectedly . For this circumstance then gave us an opportunity of judging of the course of her daily life ; a thing which especially discovers what is the disposition of each individual .

sections.label: Section 2.3.285
sections.translation: We found her industriously plying at the web ; plainly clad in a mourning dress , [*] on account of this old woman , I suppose , who was lately dead ; without golden ornaments , dressed , besides , just like those who only dress for themselves , and patched up with no worthless woman ’ s trumpery . [*]

sections.label: Section 2.3.290
sections.translation: Her hair was loose , long , and thrown back negligently about her temples . ( To CLINIA .) Do you hold your peace . [*]

sections.label: Section 2.3.291b
sections.translation: My dear Syrus , do not without cause throw me into ecstasies , I beseech you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.292b
sections.translation: The old woman was spinning the woof : [*] there was one little servant girl besides ;— she was weaving [*] together with them , covered with patched clothes ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.295
sections.translation: slovenly , and dirty with filthiness .

sections.label: Section 2.3.295b
sections.translation: If this is true , Clinia , as I believe it is , who is there more fortunate than you ? Do you mark this girl whom he speaks of , as dirty and drabbish ? This , too , is a strong indication that the mistress is out of harm ’ s way , when her confidant is in such ill plight ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.300
sections.translation: for it is a rule with those who wish to gain access to the mistress , first to bribe the maid .

sections.label: Section 2.3.302
sections.original: Perge , obsecro te , et cave ne falsam gratiam
sections.translation: Go on , I beseech you ; and beware of endeavoring to purchase favor by telling an untruth . What did she say , when you mentioned me ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.304
sections.original: Ubi dicimus redisse te , et rogare uti
sections.translation: When we told her that you had returned , and had requested her

sections.label: Section 2.3.305
sections.original: Veniret ad te , mulier telam deserit
sections.translation: to come to you , the damsel instantly put away the web , and covered her face all over with tears ; so that you might easily perceive that it really was caused by her affection for you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.308
sections.original: Prae gaudio , ita me Di ament , ubi sim nescio :
sections.translation: So may the Deities bless me , I know not where I am for joy ! I was so alarmed before .

sections.label: Section 2.3.309b
sections.original: At ego nihil esse scibam , Clinia .
sections.translation: But I was sure that there was no reason , Clinia . Come now , Syrus , tell me , in my turn , who this other lady is .

sections.label: Section 2.3.311
sections.translation: Your Bacchis , whom we are bringing . [*]

sections.label: Section 2.3.311b
sections.translation: Ha ! What ! Bacchis ? How now , you rascal ! whither are you bringing her ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.312b
sections.original: Quo ego illam ? ad nos scilicet .
sections.translation: Whither am I bringing her ? To our house , to be sure .

sections.label: Section 2.3.313
sections.translation: What ! to my father ’ s ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.313b
sections.translation: To the very same .

sections.label: Section 2.3.313c
sections.translation: Oh , the audacious impudence of the fellow !

sections.label: Section 2.3.313d
sections.translation: Hark ’ ye , no great and memorable action is done without some risk .

sections.label: Section 2.3.315
sections.original: Hoc vide : in mea vita tu tibi laudem is quaesitum , scelus ;
sections.translation: Look now ; are you seeking to gain credit for yourself , at the hazard of my character , you rascal , in a point , where , if you only make the slightest slip , I am ruined ? What would you be doing with her ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.317b
sections.translation: But still —

sections.label: Section 2.3.317c
sections.translation: Why still ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.317d
sections.translation: If you ’ ll give me leave , I ’ ll tell you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.317e
sections.translation: Do give him leave .

sections.label: Section 2.3.318
sections.translation: I give him leave then .

sections.label: Section 2.3.318b
sections.original: Ita res est haec nunc , quasi cum .—
sections.translation: This affair is now just as though when —

sections.label: Section 2.3.318c
sections.translation: Plague on it , what roundabout story is he beginning to tell me ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.319b
sections.original: Syre , verum hic dicit ; mitte : ad rem redi .
sections.translation: Syrus , he says what ’ s right — do omit digressions ; come to the point .

sections.label: Section 2.3.320
sections.translation: Really I can not hold my tongue . Clitipho , you are every way unjust , and can not possibly be endured .
